mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue momentum for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest available value was 367941.0 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with still-positive consumer demand
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward instances or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% after a tightening cycle raises equity discount rates and financing costs, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0% or remains above 5.0% after rising over the prior 12 months
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 5.24 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS episodes produce nonnegative mean 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the negative return hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI is rising while the federal funds rate is already high, inflation prevents rapid policy easing and keeps real financing conditions restrictive, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 202.3 as of t-2mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 5.24 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-CPI and high-FEDFUNDS trigger has higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-trigger periods or if interaction terms add no out-of-sample explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with slowing payroll growth marks weakening labor income and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:PAYEMS
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.8 as of t-1mo
fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 135354.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor rollover episodes are not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than baseline or if the negative return hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in industrial production signals expanding physical output and stronger cyclical earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest available value was 113.2096 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum has a 5-day forward S&P 500 Rank IC below 0.03 for any rolling 3-year window or if mean triggered returns are not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and future consumer durable demand, leading analysts to cut cyclical earnings forecasts and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest available value was 1850.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if mean triggered returns exceed unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth remains positive while CPI accelerates, nominal demand is being supported by price inflation rather than real volume, pressuring margins and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3%
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:RSAFS latest available value was 367941.0 as of t-1mo
fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 202.3 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-CPI interaction is not associated with lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than positive RSAFS growth alone or if its interaction coefficient is not negative out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate together with a high federal funds rate signals a mature expansion with limited labor slack and restrictive policy, increasing earnings and valuation downside risk over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 4.8 as of t-1mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 5.24 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion under restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE and high-FEDFUNDS episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the joint trigger underperforms neither single-series condition.
